LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT FURTH FIRM L.L.P., CHALK HILL ESTATE VINEYARDS L.L.C., CHALK HILL ESTATE WINERY L.L.C. AND FREDERICK FURTH'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR LIBEL (REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE), SUBMITTED ON MAY-22-2008 BY HAROLD E. KAHN. ARGUED AND THE COURT VACATES ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND TAKES THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, L.L.C. AND FREDERICK FURTH'S ANTI-SLAPP SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR LIBEL (REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE). ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE MOTION TO STRIKE THE LIBEL CAUSE OF ACTION IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 425.16. THE E-MAIL IS A WRITTEN STATEMENT ISSUED IN CONNECTION WITH AN ISSUE UNDER JUDICIAL REVIEW. CONTEMPORARY SERVICE CORP. V STAFFPRO INC. (2007) 152 CAL APP 4TH 1043. HAVING SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF, THE COURT REVIEWS PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE TO DETERMINE WHETHER PLAINTIFF IS LIKELY TO PREVAIL ON THE MERITS. THE COURT CONCLUDES THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT DO SO BECAUSE BOTH THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE AND THE COMMON INTEREST PRIVILEGE BAR PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM. CIVIL CODE SECTION 47; HEALY V TUSCAN HILLS LANDSCAPE & RECREATION CORP. (2006) 137 CAL APP 4TH 1, 5-6; DEAILE V GENERAL TELEGRAPH CO. (1974) 40 CAL APP 3D 841, 847. PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE MALICE SO AS TO NEGATE THE PRIVILEGE. REGARDING DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS, THE COURT NEED NOT RULE ON EACH ONE GIVEN THE COURT'S RULING ON THE PRIVILEGES. ALTHOUGH THERE IS SOME HEARSAY IN MS. BURTON'S STATEMENT, THE VAST MAJORITY OF THE OBJECTIONS ARE WITHOUT MERIT. COURT STRIKES THE ENTIRE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. AN AMENDED COMPLAINT CANNOT BE FILED WHILE AN ANTI-SLAPP MOTION IS PENDING. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Ntc Of Hearing On Dfs' Anti-Slapp Special Mtn To Strike Pl'S 1st Cause Of Action For Libel, Request For Judicial Ntc
Off Calendar Jun-06-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS FURTH FIRM, L.L.P., CHALK HILL ESTATE VINEYARDS, L.L.C., CHALK HILL ESTATE WINERY, L.L.C. AND FREDERICK FURTH'S ANTI-SLAPP SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR LIBEL (REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE).
APPEARANCES: CAROL P. LAPLANT, ESQUIRE (510.526.4325) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. DAVID M. MCLAUGHLIN, ESQUIRE (ROPERS MAJESKI, ET AL., 650.364.8200) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE MOTION TO STRIKE THE LIBEL CAUSE OF ACTION IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 425.16. THE E-MAIL IS A WRITTEN STATEMENT ISSUED IN CONNECTION WITH AN ISSUE UNDER JUDICIAL REVIEW. CONTEMPORARY SERVICE CORP. V STAFFPRO INC. (2007) 152 CAL APP 4TH 1043. HAVING SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF, THE COURT REVIEWS PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE TO DETERMINE WHETHER PLAINTIFF IS LIKELY TO PREVAIL ON THE MERITS. THE COURT CONCLUDES THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT DO SO BECAUSE BOTH THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE AND THE COMMON INTEREST PRIVILEGE BAR PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM. CIVIL CODE SECTION 47; HEALY V TUSCAN HILLS LANDSCAPE & RECREATION CORP. (2006) 137 CAL APP 4TH 1, 5-6; DEAILE V GENERAL TELEGRAPH CO. (1974) 40 CAL APP 3D 841, 847. PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE MALICE SO AS TO NEGATE THE PRIVILEGE. REGARDING DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS, THE COURT NEED NOT RULE ON EACH ONE GIVEN THE COURT'S RULING ON THE PRIVILEGES. ALTHOUGH THERE IS SOME HEARSAY IN MS. BURTON'S STATEMENT, THE VAST MAJORITY OF THE OBJECTIONS ARE WITHOUT MERIT. COURT STRIKES THE ENTIRE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. AN AMENDED COMPLAINT CANNOT BE FILED WHILE AN ANTI-SLAPP MOTION IS PENDING. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-14-2008 Continued To Jul-03-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212
